title: Air-fryer buffalo cauliflower wings
subtitle: Spiced battered florets tossed in hot sauce
excerpt: Cauliflower florets dipped in a seasoned flour batter, cooked in the air fryer at 200°C for 20 minutes until set and golden, then tossed in buffalo hot sauce and returned for 5 minutes to caramelise the glaze. The sauce goes on after the first cook, not before: batter with sauce already in it steams rather than crisps.
sourceNotes: Cauliflower wings are a vegetarian alternative to chicken wings that became popular in Britain in the 2010s through vegan restaurant menus and plant-based eating trends. The buffalo flavour (hot sauce, butter, garlic) is adapted from the original Buffalo chicken wing, invented at the Anchor Bar in Buffalo, New York, in 1964. The technique of coating and cooking before glazing is common to both versions.

content.text: The batter must be thin enough to drip slightly when a floret is lifted from the bowl. A thick batter puffs and separates from the cauliflower during cooking, leaving a hollow shell. A thin batter clings to the floret surface and forms a crisp, even coating that holds together when the hot sauce is applied.

items.prepNote: melted, or vegan butter for dairy-free

content.text: Whisk the {{plain-flour}}, {{whole-milk}}, {{garlic-powder}}, {{smoked-paprika}}, and {{sea-salt-fine}} into a thin batter. It should coat a spoon and drip off in a steady stream. Dip each {{cauliflower}} floret into the batter and let the excess drip off. Preheat the air fryer to 200°C for 3 minutes. Spray the basket with oil. Arrange the battered florets in a single layer. Spray the tops with oil. Cook at 200°C for 20 minutes, turning once at the 10-minute mark, until the batter is set, golden, and firm. While they cook, mix the {{louisiana-hot-sauce}}, melted {{unsalted-butter}}, and {{garlic-powder}} into the buffalo sauce. Transfer the cooked florets to a bowl, toss in the buffalo sauce until coated, then return to the basket. Cook for a further 4 to 5 minutes until the sauce has caramelised onto the surface.

items.symptom: Batter separating from the cauliflower during the first cook
items.cause: Batter too thick, or florets not dried before dipping
items.fix: Pat the cauliflower dry before battering. Add milk in small amounts to reach the right consistency: the batter should coat the floret and drip off slowly, not thickly. Too thick a batter puffs away from the vegetable

items.symptom: Wings not crisping on the outside
items.cause: Insufficient oil spray, or basket too full
items.fix: The batter needs oil to crisp. Spray generously before cooking and again after turning. Cook in a single layer with space between each piece

items.symptom: Buffalo sauce running off after the second cook rather than sticking
items.cause: Too much sauce, or sauce applied too hot
items.fix: Toss in just enough sauce to coat without pooling at the bottom of the bowl. The second cook in the air fryer sets and caramelises the glaze; if the sauce is running off after 5 minutes, give it 2 more minutes

content.text: Air-fryer buffalo cauliflower wings are a sharing plate or starter. Serve with celery sticks, sliced carrot, and a blue cheese or ranch dip for the full American experience. They work at a barbecue, as a game-day snack, or as a vegetarian centre plate at a casual dinner. Use vegan butter and plant-based milk to make the dish fully vegan.
